description: Classic Soulard brick townhouse on a quiet one-way street two blocks from the farmers market. Original hardwood floors, exposed brick in the kitchen, and a private rear patio. Updated electrical and plumbing.
Pre-Civil War row home in the heart of historic Soulard. Original millwork, 10-foot ceilings, and a newly repointed brick facade. Walking distance to Anheuser-Busch and the weekend market.
Solid brick bungalow on a tree-lined Tower Grove South block. Three bedrooms, dining room with built-ins, and a deep backyard with a two-car garage. New roof in 2022.

Handsome four-bedroom home on one of Shaw's most coveted blocks, steps from the Missouri Botanical Garden. Original stained glass, pocket doors, and a wraparound front porch. Fully updated kitchen and baths.

Elegant three-bedroom in the Central West End, half a block from Maryland Avenue's restaurants. Renovated chef's kitchen, primary suite with walk-in closet, and a landscaped rear garden with a flagstone patio.

Corner unit on the fourth floor of a boutique Clayton building with panoramic views of the county seat. Floor-to-ceiling windows, waterfall island, and two deeded parking spaces in the attached garage.
Contemporary two-bedroom in the Cortex innovation district, steps from Barnes-Jewish Hospital and Forest Park. Open floorplan, quartz counters, and a private balcony. One deeded parking space.

A rare full-floor residence in one of St. Louis's most prestigious private streets. Formal dining room, three fireplaces, staff quarters, and a climate-controlled wine room. Two indoor parking spaces and private storage.

Grand Italianate on Lafayette Square's most photographed block. Four bedrooms, parlor with original plaster medallions, updated kitchen with marble counters, and a carriage house converted to a studio.
Gated estate on 1.8 acres in Ladue with a heated pool, full outdoor kitchen, and a six-car garage. Primary suite with private terrace, home theater, and a wine cellar. Extensively renovated in 2021.
End-unit townhouse on the border of Benton Park and Soulard with a rooftop deck and two-car garage. Open main level, primary suite on the top floor, and a finished basement bonus room.
Neat brick bungalow in Dogtown, one of the city's most stable and sought-after neighborhoods. Original woodwork, covered front porch, updated kitchen, and a detached garage with alley access.

Fully occupied two-family flat on Cherokee Street's prime antique row. Two three-bedroom units, each with separate utilities. Current gross rents of $2,800/month. Strong cap rate in a rapidly appreciating corridor.

Four-bedroom craftsman two blocks from the Kirkwood train station and downtown shops. Covered front porch, built-in bookshelves in the study, updated baths, and a private backyard with mature shade trees.

Updated three-bedroom in Old Webster, steps from Gore Avenue's boutiques and coffee shops. Hardwood floors throughout, open kitchen with island, and a landscaped backyard with a deck. Top-rated Webster Groves schools.
